exemplar.title:
To what extent the LGBTIQ+ population as a social movement influenced the
Colombian State politics between 2016 and 2021?

detailedExemplarData.title:
To what extent the LGBTIQ+ population as a social movement influenced the
Colombian State politics between 2016 and 2021?

strengths: Clear and sharply focused research question with defined temporal and geographical scope
Comprehensive definitions of key concepts directly linked to the case study
Well-stated objectives and a coherent qualitative methodology outlining relevant source types
weaknesses: Occasional digressions to global examples without explicit linkage back to the Colombian LGBTIQ+ case
Minor linguistic inaccuracies that slightly impede seamless exposition
Limited use of primary data or contrasting scholarly interpretations to deepen methodological rigor
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Based on the strand assessments (all at band 5/6), the student communicates the topic accurately and formulates a sharply focused question, while presenting a complete methodology. Minor lapses in focus and limited methodological breadth prevent a top-band score. A mark of 5/6 appropriately reflects a high-quality performance with room for refinement.

strengths: Effective integration of primary legal documents and NGO reports to substantiate key points
Coherent application of social-movement theories (Touraine, Almeida) to the empirical case
Accurate and mostly consistent use of discipline-specific terminology
weaknesses: Some narrative sections lack direct linkage to the research question and dilute focus
Insufficient citation precision (missing page references, reliance on secondary news summaries)
Inconsistent date references and occasional undefined concepts (e.g., ânegative peaceâ)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work demonstrates clear knowledge with mostly relevant application of sources and generally accurate terminology, aligning with band 3â4 descriptors. Persistent lapses in synthesis and citation accuracy limit this to a mid-band score of 4/6.

strengths: Analysis grounded in primary legal rulings (C-358, T-068, SC128-2018) adding research depth
Use of quantitative implementation data to evaluate state effectiveness
Compelling case study (Anyela Ramos) illustrating concrete movement influence
weaknesses: Predominantly descriptive narrative with limited systematic evaluation of broader impacts
Insufficient critical engagement with theoretical models and frameworks
Comparative examples (Arab Spring, BLM) are not consistently tied back to the core research question
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand scores (research quality 5/12, analysis 4/12, evaluation 4/12) place performance in the mid-band (4â6). While the essay shows good evidence use and partial critical evaluation, it lacks the sustained, critical depth for higher marks. A holistic score of 5/12 balances strengths and weaknesses.

strengths: Clear and appropriate overall structure following EE conventions
Consistent use of headings and logical progression supporting reader understanding
weaknesses: Layout inconsistencies (superscripts, pagination markers) disrupt readability
Section and subsection numbering is sometimes mismatched
Dense paragraphs and uneven reference formatting hinder ease of navigation
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The structure fully supports comprehension (band 3â4), but multiple layout and formatting issues prevent a top-band score. A mark of 3/4 accurately reflects mostly appropriate presentation with notable areas for improvement.

strandSelections.reasoning: The essay goes beyond narrative by contrasting movement demands with state responses and implementation data, but much of the text strings together events chronologically rather than interrogating causal mechanisms.  Assertions (e.g., âpartial influenceâ) are supported, yet supporting logic is sometimes thin, placing analysis at the lower end of the 4-6 band.

The bibliography shows a solid mix of legal documents, NGO reports and academic texts, reflecting adequate breadth.  Nearly all directly address Colombian LGBTIQ+ rights or social-movement theory, demonstrating relevance.  A few peripheral sources (Arab Spring, BLM) dilute focus, keeping the work in the mid-band.

The essay follows an orthodox EE structure (title page, abstract substitute, TOC, introduction, three-section body, conclusion, references) that clearly supports reader understanding.  Headings match the TOC and progression is logical, warranting the top mark in this band.

The student demonstrates clear knowledge of Colombian politics, the peace process and social-movement theory (Touraine, Almeida).  Connections between theory and empirical case studies are made, showing coherence.  Nonetheless, treatment remains descriptive in places and lacks the depth of specialist scholarship that would merit the highest band.

The student offers an evaluative conclusion, acknowledges data scarcity and judges the movementâs influence as âpartialâ.  Some critique of state effectiveness is included (implementation 66 %/33 % figures), but systematic weighing of alternative explanations or counter-arguments is limited.  Evaluation is therefore âpartialâ rather than âcriticalâ.

Title, word count and table are present; APA-style in-text citations are used and the bibliography is alphabetised.  Minor issuesâMath-style superscripts in the text, inconsistent page numbering indications (e.g., â{5}â)âshow that layout was not flawlessly applied, so 3 is appropriate.

Terminology such as ânegative peaceâ, âDe Facto Marital Unionâ, âdifferential approachâ and âSOGIGEâ is used accurately and repeatedly.  Occasional grammatical slips (e.g., â$LGBTIO+$â) and inconsistent acronyms slightly impede clarity, so the performance fits the âmostly accurateâ descriptor.

The student states the purpose, temporal boundaries (2016-2021) and key concepts (influence, social movement) clearly in the opening page, then reiterates them in the introduction and conclusion.  Definitions of social movements, negative peace and vulnerability are supplied and linked directly to the Colombian LGBTIQ+ case, allowing readers to understand the scope without ambiguity.  Minor linguistic inaccuracies and occasional digressions (Arab Spring, BLM) prevent the exposition from being fully seamless, hence the award of 5 rather than 6.

A qualitative, secondary-source methodology is declared; official documents, court rulings, NGO reports and academic texts are used.  The range is appropriate for a GP EE and sources are largely selected for their direct relevance to the question.  However, primary data or contrasting scholarly interpretations are limited, so the methodology, while complete, is not exemplary.

The research question is explicit, specific to one movement, one state and a fixed five-year window, evidencing sharp focus.  Objectives are set out (âcharacteriseâ, âanalyse effortsâ, âcontrast with effectivenessâ) and are followed consistently throughout the body.  Focus slips slightly when global comparators are inserted without being tied back to the question, so a top-band 6 is not justified.

Source material (court sentences, Colombia Diversa reports, peace-agreement text) is integrated to substantiate points on rights recognition and implementation gaps.  Citations are generally accurate; nevertheless several paragraphs lapse into narrative description with scant linkage to the research question, and some sources (news sites) are quoted at length without synthesis, so application is âmostlyâ rather than âconsistentlyâ relevant.

annotations.comment: This dense paragraph lists multiple state actions; split into shorter paragraphs to improve readability and highlight distinct elements.
Consider adding subâsubsection numbering (e.g., 2.3.1, 2.3.2) within long sections to guide the reader through complex content.
Detailed descriptions of key legal rulings (C-358, T-068, SC128-2018) ground the argument in primary sources, enhancing research quality.
The narrative recounts SC128-2018 in detail but stops short of evaluating its broader systemic impact; add analysis of longâterm implications.
The research question is clearly stated, specific to one movement, one state, and a fixed period, providing sharp focus from the outset.
Table of contents uses âp.p.â inconsistent with standard pagination; revise to simple page numbers (e.g., â3â6â) for clarity.
The introduction uses the informal contraction âitâsâ; revise to formal academic tone (e.g., âone of these is the rightâ¦â).
The claim about sameâsex marriage progress lacks a citation for key dates and country counts; add authoritative sources to support this overview.
This paragraph narrates global marriage reforms descriptively without linking insights back to the Colombian LGBTIQ+ context; anchor analysis to the research question.
Effective use of UNICEFâs Social and Behavior Change guidance provides a solid conceptual foundation for defining social movements.
Assertion about guerrilla and state harassment requires specific sourcing to validate claims and strengthen credibility.
Long quotation from CastaÃ±eda (2016) is presented without critical engagement; condense and analyze its implications for the argument.
The Arab Spring and BLM examples enrich context but need explicit linkage back to the Colombian LGBTIQ+ case to maintain focus.
Global comparators (Arab Spring, BLM) broaden the discussion but dilute the focus on LGBTIQ+ influence; tie each comparative example back to the research aims.
The statement of objectives and qualitative methodology is clear and appropriately outlines source types and analytical focus.
Section heading â2. BODYâ does not match numbered TOC style; use consistent formatting and numbering for subheadings.
Applying Touraineâs tripartite model to the LGBTIQ+ case illustrates strong theoretical integration and enriches conceptual understanding.
The theoretical application remains descriptive; introduce critique of Touraineâs model and assess its limits in the Colombian context.
Almeida citation lacks page references; provide precise locators and discuss potential limitations of his framework.
Use of quantitative implementation data (66% vs. 33%) provides analytical depth in assessing state effectiveness.
Almeidaâs concept of an excluded collectivity is well explained and linked to LGBTIQ+ strategies, displaying coherent theoretical use.
Description of Red Somos programs ends abruptly; expand explanation of how each program tangibly impacts rights recognition.
Direct quotes from the Peace Agreement substantiate the movementâs state recognition; this integration of primary legal text strengthens the argument.
Reliance on a VLex news summary for court decisions is insufficient; reference official court transcripts or academic analyses for robustness.
Subsection heading formatting is too similar to body text; differentiate via font or numbering to guide the reader effectively.
Case study of Anyela Ramos is compelling and illustrates the movementâs concrete influence on legal precedence.
Date references are inconsistent (MuÃ±oz, 2020 vs. 2021 strike); verify and correct timeline to maintain credibility.
Rephrase numerical comparisons for clarity (e.g., â109 murders in 2018, one fewer than 2017â) to improve readability.
The discussion of demands versus state responses shows depth; conclusions about positive and negative outcomes reveal partial critical evaluation.
Synthesis of Touraine and Almeida in the conclusion effectively connects theory and evidence, showing strong evaluative skills.
Inclusion of the Truth Commission report demonstrates comprehensive sourcing and depth of research on conflict impacts.
Introducing ânegative peaceâ is promising but requires a clear definition; briefly explain Smorkerâs concept before application.
Consider adding a brief reflection on methodological limitations and data scarcity in the conclusion to deepen critical insight.
Some reference entries wrap inconsistently, causing uneven layout; reformat text blocks to maintain alignment.
The inclusion of a word count meets the requirement and confirms adherence to the maximum limit, demonstrating attention to formatting guidelines.
The âIB codeâ field lacks clarity on its purpose; consider labeling explicitly (e.g., Candidate Number, Session) and including cohort details.
Footnote formatting is inconsistent; adopt a standardized superscript style and ensure alignment with citation guidelines.
The overline notation appears to be an OCR artifact; remove â\overline{7}â for clarity.
Author details for MuÃ±oz (2020) lack initials; include full author name or initials to adhere to citation standards.
Reference entries should follow consistent APA formatting, including italics for report titles and proper retrieval dates.
Ensure URLs are not split across lines in the bibliography to maintain accessibility and clickable links.
